entry.title: [FT] Tim Britton Ranks Every MLB Team's Trade Deadline Strategy
entry.summary:
The Athletic senior writer Tim Britton joins the show to rank every MLB team by its trade deadline approach and explain which clubs should be buying, selling or stuck in the middle. He discusses why teams like the Brewers, Yankees and Pirates should be aggressive, identifies the biggest swing teams that could reshape the deadline, and breaks down which players could become the market's biggest trade chips over the next two weeks.
See how K12 Powered Schools can help unlock your child’s full potential. Enroll online today at https://K12.com/foul
Subscribe to the podcast! https://apple.co/3KWKFNo https://open.spotify.com/show/4NCHAElLW0pR2sclRnqj0V
Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow
entry.content:
The Athletic senior writer Tim Britton joins the show to rank every MLB team by its trade deadline approach and explain which clubs should be buying, selling or stuck in the middle. He discusses why teams like the Brewers, Yankees and Pirates should be aggressive, identifies the biggest swing teams that could reshape the deadline, and breaks down which players could become the market's biggest trade chips over the next two weeks.

entry.title: [FT] Former MLB GM Jim Bowden Breaks Down His Boldest Trade Ideas
entry.summary:
FT Resident GM Jim Bowden joins the show to share five bold trade ideas he'd like to see before the MLB Trade Deadline, including potential deals involving George Kirby, Hunter Goodman, Michael Wacha and Luis Arraez. Bowden also explains why he believes the Blue Jays should become sellers, discusses where Kevin Gausman and Daulton Varsho could fit, and weighs in on MLB's push for a salary cap and how it could reshape the league.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow
entry.content:
FT Resident GM Jim Bowden joins the show to share five bold trade ideas he'd like to see before the MLB Trade Deadline, including potential deals involving George Kirby, Hunter Goodman, Michael Wacha and Luis Arraez. Bowden also explains why he believes the Blue Jays should become sellers, discusses where Kevin Gausman and Daulton Varsho could fit, and weighs in on MLB's push for a salary cap and how it could reshape the league.

entry.title: [FT] The Blue Jays Can't Ignore Reality Anymore
entry.summary:
AJ Pierzynski and Erik Kratz react to another rough loss for the Blue Jays and debate whether Toronto has reached the point where selling at the trade deadline is unavoidable. They discuss Kevin Gausman's future, whether Daulton Varsho and other veterans should be on the trade block, and break down several intriguing mock trades that could reshape the franchise.
Get 20% off your first order of $125 or more at https://MackWeldon.com with promo code FOUL

Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow

entry.title: [FT] Should These MLB Stars Be Traded At The Deadline?
entry.summary:
Kevin Pillar, Todd Frazier and Tricia Whitaker spin the trade deadline wheel to debate whether some of MLB's biggest names should stay put or be moved before the deadline. They weigh in on Kevin Gausman, CJ Abrams, Tarik Skubal, Joe Ryan, José Soriano, Seiya Suzuki and more while making the case for buyers and sellers across the league. Plus, the trio predicts which teams should cash in now and which contenders should hold onto their biggest stars.
See how K12 Powered Schools can help unlock your child’s full potential. Enroll online today at https://K12.com/foul

Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow
entry.content:

They weigh in on Kevin Gausman, CJ Abrams, Tarik Skubal, Joe Ryan, José Soriano, Seiya Suzuki and more while making the case for buyers and sellers across the league. entry.title: [FT] AJ Pierzynski & Erik Kratz Debate MLB's Top 10 Teams Right Now
entry.summary:
AJ Pierzynski and Erik Kratz reveal their latest MLB power rankings and explain which teams climbed, fell and dropped out since their last edition. The two debate where contenders like the Dodgers, Brewers, Braves, Phillies, Yankees and Rays belong, make the case for the surging White Sox and Red Sox, and discuss which teams could shake up the rankings with a big move before the trade deadline.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Shares What He's Hearing About MLB's Latest Trade Candidates
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal joins the show to discuss his latest report on three All-Star catchers who have emerged as trade candidates ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline. Ken also shares the latest on the Braves, Red Sox, Padres and Blue Jays, breaks down Shohei Ohtani's pitching timeline, and explains whether the Dodgers could be in the market for another starting pitcher.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Explains How The Trade Deadline Is Starting To Take Shape
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal discusses why so many teams are still undecided ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline and what that means for buyers and sellers across the league. Ken breaks down the latest on Francisco Lindor and the Mets, why the Reds are open for business, and what makes the Nationals one of the most intriguing teams to watch over the next two weeks. Plus, he explains why the crowded National League Wild Card race could keep the trade market in flux right up until the deadline.

entry.content:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal discusses why so many teams are still undecided ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline and what that means for buyers and sellers across the league. Ken breaks down the latest on Francisco Lindor and the Mets, why the Reds are open for business, and what makes the Nationals one of the most intriguing teams to watch over the next two weeks. Plus, he explains why the crowded National League Wild Card race could keep the trade market in flux right up until the deadline.

entry.title: [FT] MLB Insider Bob Nightengale Reveals What He's Hearing Before The Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
USA TODAY MLB insider Bob Nightengale joins the show to share the latest trade deadline buzz from around baseball, including the Dodgers, Padres, Mets, Braves and Tigers. Bob explains why Los Angeles could pursue catching help, weighs in on Francisco Lindor's future in New York, and discusses how Atlanta's rotation plans could change with Spencer Strider's uncertain status. Plus, he shares what he's hearing about several contenders and sellers as the trade deadline rapidly approaches.

entry.title: [FT] Tim Healey Breaks Down The Red Sox Epic Win Streak & Trade Deadline Outlook
entry.summary:
Boston Globe Red Sox beat writer Tim Healey joins the show to explain what's fueling Boston's remarkable winning streak and why everything has clicked over the past month. He provides the latest on Roman Anthony's recovery, discusses Chad Tracy's impact since taking over as interim manager, and breaks down how the Red Sox should approach the trade deadline. Plus, Healey shares why the rotation has carried Boston through Garrett Crochet's absence and what the timeline looks like for their ace's return.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] Breaking Down Shohei Ohtani's Latest Injury Update & The Dodgers Deadline Outlook
entry.summary:
Todd Frazier, Kevin Pillar and Tricia Whitaker discuss Dave Roberts latest update on Shohei Ohtani after the Dodgers delayed his next start with no timetable for his return to the mound. They explain what the decision means for Ohtani's Cy Young chances and debate who should emerge as the NL Cy Young favorite if he can't get back on track. Plus, they break down whether Los Angeles should be more aggressive at the trade deadline with Will Smith sidelined and questions surrounding the Dodgers rotation.
See how K12 Powered Schools can help unlock your child’s full potential. Enroll online today at https://K12.com/foul

Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow

entry.title: [FT] 4 Teams That Could Shape The MLB Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
Who should buy, who should sell, and who should stand pat at the MLB Trade Deadline? Alanna Rizzo, Todd Frazier and Erik Kratz play a game of "Wheeling and Dealing," breaking down the Mets, Blue Jays, Padres and Tigers while debating the biggest decisions each team faces before the deadline. Plus, the crew discusses potential trade targets, whether Tarik Skubal should be moved, and what could shape the market over the next two weeks.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Breaks Down The Hottest MLB Trade Rumors Ahead Of The Deadline

We discuss Francisco Lindor, the Giants' biggest trade questions, the Braves' deadline priorities, Tarik Skubal's future, and which contenders could make the biggest moves before the deadline.

entry.title: [FT] Phillies Trade Deadline Plans Just Changed
entry.summary:
The Phillies suffered a major blow with the loss of Brad Keller for the rest of the season, creating an even bigger need ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline. Alanna Rizzo, Todd Frazier and Erik Kratz discuss how Keller's injury changes Dave Dombrowski's deadline priorities, whether Philadelphia should prioritize bullpen help over a right-handed bat, and which trade targets could help the Phillies make another postseason run.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] How Aggressive Should The White Sox Be At The Trade Deadline?
entry.summary:
James Fegan of Sox Machine joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun following the announcement that Sox Machine is officially joining the Foul Territory Network! We discuss the surprising first-place White Sox, why Chicago's turnaround happened sooner than expected, and what the club should do at the trade deadline. Plus, James breaks down the White Sox's biggest roster needs, whether Reid Detmers is a realistic trade target, and which young players could become the foundation of the franchise for years to come.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] MLB Insider Robert Murray Shares The Latest Trade Deadline Buzz
entry.summary:
MLB insider Robert Murray of FanSided joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun to break down the latest trade deadline buzz around baseball. We discuss the Padres' plans with Mason Miller, the Brewers' trade for Lance McCullers Jr., which teams could become the biggest buyers and sellers, and why Tarik Skubal could still be the biggest name to watch before the deadline. Plus, Robert shares the latest on the Phillies' trade targets, the White Sox's surprising position as buyers, and a few under-the-radar players who could end up on the move.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount
0:00 Mason Miller 1:55 Lance McCullers to Brewers 4:00 Giants 7:50 White Sox & Reid Detmers 11:53 Jung Hoo Lee 14:25 Freddy Peralta 15:15 Is parody good for the deadline? 17:00 GM’s treating it different 18:05 Phillies 20:00 Robert’s new subtext
Subscribe to the podcast! https://apple.co/3KWKFNo https://open.spotify.com/show/4NCHAElLW0pR2sclRnqj0V
Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow
entry.content:
MLB insider Robert Murray of FanSided joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun to break down the latest trade deadline buzz around baseball. We discuss the Padres' plans with Mason Miller, the Brewers' trade for Lance McCullers Jr., which teams could become the biggest buyers and sellers, and why Tarik Skubal could still be the biggest name to watch before the deadline. Plus, Robert shares the latest on the Phillies' trade targets, the White Sox's surprising position as buyers, and a few under-the-radar players who could end up on the move.

Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

1:55 Lance McCullers to Brewers
4:00 Giants
7:50 White Sox & Reid Detmers
11:53 Jung Hoo Lee
14:25 Freddy Peralta
15:15 Is parody good for the deadline?
17:00 GM’s treating it different
18:05 Phillies
20:00 Robert’s new subtext

entry.title: [FT] BREAKING: Reds Lock Up Chase Burns Long-Term
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun break down Chase Burns' seven-year extension with the Reds and what it means for both sides. We discuss why more young pitchers are choosing long-term security over free agency, how Cincinnati has built one of baseball's best young rotations, and why the organization's investment in pitching development is paying off. Plus, we explain why this deal could become a blueprint for small-market teams moving forward.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] These MLB Teams Changed Our Expectations For The Second Half
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun discuss the biggest surprises heading into the second half of the MLB season. They break down the Red Sox incredible turnaround, debate whether Boston should be buyers at the trade deadline, and explain what's gone wrong for the Athletics after their stunning collapse. Plus, the guys share their thoughts on the first-place White Sox, the Cubs playoff chances, and several under-the-radar teams that could make noise after the All-Star break.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] Cole Hamels On The 2008 Phillies, Zack Wheeler & This Year's World Series Hopes
entry.summary:
Former MLB All-Star Cole Hamels joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Hamels reflects on the 2008 Phillies, explains why he still roots for the franchise, and shares behind-the-scenes stories from one of the greatest rotations in baseball. Plus, he weighs in on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, discusses how starting pitchers should be developed in today's game, and looks back on some unforgettable moments from his Phillies career.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount​

entry.title: [FT] MLB All-Star Red Carpet Interviews | Paul Skenes, Bryce Harper, Munetaka Murakami & More
entry.summary:
Alanna Rizzo and Tricia Whitaker catch up with some of baseball's biggest stars from the MLB All-Star Game red carpet in Philadelphia! Paul Skenes, Livvy Dunne, Munetaka Murakami, Bryce Harper, Juan Soto, Dave Roberts, Freddie Freeman, Max Muncy, Ozzie Albies, Drake Baldwin, Oli Marmol and CJ Abrams stop by to discuss All-Star Week, the Home Run Derby, memorable moments from the first half, and what they're looking forward to during the Midsummer Classic.

Alanna Rizzo and Tricia Whitaker catch up with some of baseball's biggest stars from the MLB All-Star Game red carpet in Philadelphia! Paul Skenes, Livvy Dunne, Munetaka Murakami, Bryce Harper, Juan Soto, Dave Roberts, Freddie Freeman, Max Muncy, Ozzie Albies, Drake Baldwin, Oli Marmol and CJ Abrams stop by to discuss All-Star Week, the Home Run Derby, memorable moments from the first half, and what they're looking forward to during the Midsummer Classic.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal On The New Derby Format, Mike Trout In Philly & Trade Deadline Outlook
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Ken breaks down the new Home Run Derby format, explains why Philadelphia is the ideal host for the All-Star Game, and discusses what kind of reception Mike Trout could receive from the hometown crowd. Plus, he shares the latest trade deadline buzz surrounding the Tigers, Red Sox, Orioles and Yankees, and weighs in on Aaron Judge's injury.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal On How MLB's Crowded Playoff Race Will Impact The Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal breaks down how MLB's crowded playoff races could completely reshape this year's trade deadline. With 23 of 30 teams still within four games of a playoff spot entering the All-Star break, Ken explains why buyers and sellers are harder to identify than ever. Plus, he discusses what the Tigers should do with Tarik Skubal, why the Red Sox have changed the conversation, and how teams like the Astros, Blue Jays, Orioles, Diamondbacks and Padres could approach one of the most unpredictable deadlines in recent memory.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount
Subscribe to the podcast! https://apple.co/3KWKFNo https://open.spotify.com/show/4NCHAElLW0pR2sclRnqj0V

entry.title: [FT] 10x All-Star Chris Sale Shares Hilarious Stories From His White Sox Days With AJ
entry.summary:
Braves ace Chris Sale joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from MLB All-Star Week in Philadelphia! Sale reflects on earning his 10th All-Star selection, looks back on his first Midsummer Classic alongside legends like Derek Jeter, Justin Verlander and David Ortiz, and discusses what it means to still be pitching at an elite level. Plus, Sale shares his thoughts on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, reveals whether he still has another gear on the mound, and tells hilarious stories from his early days with A.J. in Chicago.
See how K12 Powered Schools can help unlock your child’s full potential. Enroll online today at https://K12.com/foul

entry.title: [FT] Max Muncy on His All-Star Experience, Ohtani's Knee Injury & The Dodgers Confidence
entry.summary:
Dodgers third baseman Max Muncy joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Muncy shares what it's like preparing for another All-Star Game, explains why he turned down the Home Run Derby, and reveals why he thinks one of the hometown Phillies has the edge to win it. Plus, he provides an update on Shohei Ohtani's knee, discusses the Dodgers confidence despite a rough finish before the break, and reflects on celebrating another World Series title with a White House visit.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.title: [FT] Full Home Run Derby Preview: Predictions, Sleepers & New Rules
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun preview the 2026 Home Run Derby from Philadelphia and break down MLB's new format. The guys debate whether the changes will improve the event, discuss which hitters are best suited for Citizens Bank Park, and share their favorite picks to take home the trophy. Plus, they explain why Kyle Schwarber, Bryce Harper, Junior Caminero and Jac Caglianone could thrive under the new rules, while A.J. offers his own ideas to make the Derby even more exciting.
Download MLB 9 Innings 26 free in your app store. The ultimate baseball experience with MLB's biggest stars!
Download MLB Perfect Inning 26 in your app store today. Enjoy fantastic baseball with the latest updated MLB 2026 players!

entry.title: [FT] Former Home Run Derby Champ Todd Frazier Shares His Strategy & Predictions
entry.summary:
Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun break down the new Home Run Derby format and debate who it helps the most. Todd shares what it was really like winning the Derby, explains why hitting early could be a major advantage, and reveals the strategy he'd use under this year's rules. Plus, the guys make their Home Run Derby winner predictions, discuss why Kyle Schwarber and Junior Caminero are strong favorites, and even debate whether MLB should let players swing aluminum bats in the Derby.

entry.title: [FT] A.J. Pierzynski Shares Everything That Happened When He Met Pope Leo XIV
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski shares the unforgettable story of meeting Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican, reflecting on what the once-in-a-lifetime experience meant to him and his family. A.J. explains how the special visit came together, reveals the unique White Sox keepsake he gifted the Pope, and shares the Pope's reaction when he received it. Plus, A.J. opens up about the moment that left him starstruck, what their conversation was really like, and why he'll never forget the experience.

entry.title: [FT] Did The Twins Owner Just Admit What's Wrong With The Franchise?
entry.summary:
Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to revealing comments from Twins owner Tom Pohlad about the franchise's approach to spending and building a winner. The trio explains why Minnesota's payroll decisions have frustrated fans, debates whether ownership should invest before expecting results, and discusses what the Twins need to do to become a consistent contender. Plus, they weigh in on the club's failed sale, declining payroll and why Twins fans deserve better.
See how K12 Powered Schools can help unlock your child’s full potential. Enroll online today at https://K12.com/foul

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Shares An Aaron Judge Update & New Trade Deadline Intel
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal joins Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun to break down the latest MLB trade deadline buzz. Ken shares the latest on Aaron Judge's injury and what it could mean for the Yankees' deadline plans, explains why the Marlins now expect to keep Sandy Alcantara while adding to their roster, and discusses how Munetaka Murakami's return impacts the White Sox. Plus, Ken weighs in on the Orioles and Red Sox trade deadline outlooks and makes the case for why Hunter Goodman deserves a spot in the Home Run Derby.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount
Subscribe to the podcast! https://apple.co/3KWKFNo https://open.spotify.com/show/4NCHAElLW0pR2sclRnqj0V
Follow all our social media channels for more content! https://twitter.com/FoulTerritoryTV https://www.instagram.com/foulterritoryshow/ https://www.tiktok.com/@foulterritory https://www.facebook.com/FoulTerritoryShow
entry.content:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal joins Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun to break down the latest MLB trade deadline buzz. Ken shares the latest on Aaron Judge's injury and what it could mean for the Yankees' deadline plans, explains why the Marlins now expect to keep Sandy Alcantara while adding to their roster, and discusses how Munetaka Murakami's return impacts the White Sox. Plus, Ken weighs in on the Orioles and Red Sox trade deadline outlooks and makes the case for why Hunter Goodman deserves a spot in the Home Run Derby.

entry.title: [FT] Breaking Down The 2026 Home Run Derby Field
entry.summary:
Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react after Don Mattingly accidentally let it slip that Kyle Schwarber was headed to the Home Run Derby - just moments before the Phillies made it official. They discuss what Schwarber's addition means for this year's field, debate the final Derby spot, and make the case for names like Munetaka Murakami, James Wood and Hunter Goodman. Plus, Todd shares why the Home Run Derby is one of baseball's best events and explains how this year's new format could completely change the competition.
Use our code for 10% off your next order of MLB tickets on SeatGeek*: https://seatgeek.onelink.me/RrnK/TERRITORY10 Sponsored by SeatGeek. *Restrictions apply. Max $20 discount

entry.content:
Todd Frazier, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react after Don Mattingly accidentally let it slip that Kyle Schwarber was headed to the Home Run Derby - just moments before the Phillies made it official. They discuss what Schwarber's addition means for this year's field, debate the final Derby spot, and make the case for names like Munetaka Murakami, James Wood and Hunter Goodman. Plus, Todd shares why the Home Run Derby is one of baseball's best events and explains how this year's new format could completely change the competition.

entry.title: [FT] Ken Rosenthal Breaks Down The Trade Market For Starting Pitching
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal and Alanna Rizzo break down the starting pitching trade market ahead of the MLB trade deadline. Ken explains why Tarik Skubal, Sonny Gray, Luis Castillo, Jacob deGrom and Seth Lugo are all in unique situations, which contenders could make a move, and why so few teams have committed to buying or selling. Plus, Ken discusses why this year's trade deadline could produce more creative deals than ever before as clubs continue to navigate a crowded playoff race.
Shop now at https://Fabletics.com/Foul and get 70 to 80% off everything when you sign up as a new VIP.

entry.content:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal and Alanna Rizzo break down the starting pitching trade market ahead of the MLB trade deadline. Ken explains why Tarik Skubal, Sonny Gray, Luis Castillo, Jacob deGrom and Seth Lugo are all in unique situations, which contenders could make a move, and why so few teams have committed to buying or selling. Plus, Ken discusses why this year's trade deadline could produce more creative deals than ever before as clubs continue to navigate a crowded playoff race.
